*Description:*
Charleville Castle is a Gothic-style castle located in County Offaly, Ireland, bordering the town of Tullamore, near the River Clodiagh. It is considered one of the finest of its type in the country.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Kinnitty Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* the heartlands
*County:* County Offaly
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th-19th century
*Coords:* 53.1, -7.7
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kinnitty_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Kinnitty%20Castle/@53.068,-7.72,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/IMG_KinnittyCastle0973.jpg/330px-IMG_KinnittyCastle0973.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* https://www.kinnittycastlehotel.com/ add source, tag as hotel 

*Description:*
Kinnitty Castle or Castle Bernard is a 19th-century gothic revival castle and hotel in Kinnitty, County Offaly, Ireland. It is located contiguous to Droughtville and Lettybrook, north of the Slieve Bloom Mountains on the R421 regional road between the villages of Kinnitty and Cadamstown. A nearby pyramid was built by the Bernard family who resided in the castle.

_Listing 3 of 53_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Rathcroghan
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Roscommon
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Bronze Age-Iron Age
*Coords:* 53.802, -8.304
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rathcroghan
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Rathcroghan/@53.8,-8.303,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/rathcroghan.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* more research 

*Description:*
Rathcroghan is a complex of archaeological sites near Tulsk in County Roscommon, Ireland. It is identified as the site of Cruachan, the traditional capital of the Connachta, the prehistoric and early historic rulers of the western territory. The Rathcroghan Complex is an archaeological landscape with many references found in early Irish medieval manuscripts.

_Listing 4 of 53_

listing.name: Rathcroghan
listing.desc: Rathcroghan is a complex of archaeological sites near Tulsk in County Roscommon, Ireland. It is identified as the site of Cruachan, the traditional capital of the Connachta, the prehistoric and early historic rulers of the western territory. The Rathcroghan Complex is an archaeological landscape with many references found in early Irish medieval manuscripts.

*Name:* Donegal Abbey
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Donegal
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 54.6504, -8.1152
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donegal_Abbey
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Donegal%20Abbey/@54.651,-8.118,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/donegal-abbey.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* https://abbeyofdonegal.ie/ add source

*Description:*
Donegal Abbey is a ruined Franciscan Priory in Donegal in Ireland. It was constructed by the O'Donnell dynasty in the fifteenth century and remained a center of Classical Christian education even after its destruction during the Nine Years War. It is sometimes referred to as Donegal Friary.

_Listing 5 of 53_

It is sometimes referred to as Donegal Friary.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Springhill House
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Derry
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 17th century
*Coords:* 54.686, -6.656
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Springhill_House
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Springhill%20House/@54.747,-6.718,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/springhill-house.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Springhill is a 17th-century plantation house in the townland of Ballindrum near Moneymore, County Londonderry in Northern Ireland. It has been the property of the National Trust since 1957 and, in addition to the house, gardens and park, there is a costume collection.
It is open from March to June, and September on weekends, and is open to the public seven days a week during July and August.

_Listing 7 of 53_

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Dunserverick Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* ulster and the north
*County:* County Antrim
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Medieval
*Coords:* 55.238369, -6.44823
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dunseverick_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Dunserverick%20Castle/@55.237,-6.122,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/dunserverick-castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Dunseverick Castle is built of basalt and is situated in County Antrim, Northern Ireland, near the small village of Dunseverick and the Giant's Causeway. Dunseverick Castle and earthworks are Scheduled Historic Monuments in the townland of Feigh, in Causeway coast and Glens district council, at grid ref: C9871 4467.

_Listing 8 of 53_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Abbey Knockmoy
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* wild atlantic way
*County:* County Galway
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 53.47, -8.62
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Knockmoy_Abbey
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Abbey%20Knockmoy/@53.47,-8.62,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/abbey-knockmoy.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* https://maps.app.goo.gl/7xNzKjV54zGGn1aD6 update google places link 


*Description:*
Abbeyknockmoy is a village and parish in County Galway, Ireland. It is known for the nearby ruins of the 12th century Cistercian abbey, established with the Kings of Connacht as its benefactors. The abbey was the burial site of King Cathal Crobhdearg Ua Conchobair and contains several examples of medieval wall paintings and sculpture. It was formerly part of the kingdom of the Soghain of Connacht.

_Listing 9 of 53_
